leftPane.noItems: No codes yet. Highlight text to create codes.

centerPane.placeholder: Load a file to start analysis.

rightPane.noMemos: No memos yet. Select a code to add memos.

mindmap.title: Code Map
mindmap.noData: Create codes first to visualize the mind map.

categorymap.noData: Create categories first to visualize the category map.

memomap.title: Memo Map

usage.title: How to Use
usage.btnImport: Load a file into the project
usage.btnSave: Save the project as a .mqda file
usage.btnExport: Export codes as a CSV file
usage.btnMap: Open Code Map / Category Map / Memo Map
usage.panelFilesDesc: List of loaded files. Click to switch the active file
usage.panelCodesDesc: Hierarchical code tree. Drag & drop to reorganize
usage.panelViewerDesc: Main view for reading files and coding text
usage.panelMemosDesc: Manage and edit memos linked to codes
usage.codingDesc: Select text in the Viewer to choose between two coding modes:
usage.codingInVivoDesc: The selected text becomes the code name automatically
usage.codingInterpretiveDesc: Enter a custom code name of your choice
usage.saveResumeDesc: To pause your work, click Save to download a .mqda file. Next time, import the .mqda file via the Import button to restore all files, codes, and memos and resume where you left off.

guide.title: Qualitative Analysis Workflow
guide.goal: The goal of qualitative analysis is to discover theory from data. Through the steps below, you progressively elevate concrete text into abstract theory.
guide.memoDesc: Writing memos at every step is essential. Recording background context, insights, and reasoning behind your codes enriches the analysis and strengthens its depth.
guide.hint: Code in Viewer → Organize codes, groups & categories → Analyze relations in Maps
